outlets.name: NPR
outlets.headline: Iran war enters its fourth week with no clear end in sight
outlets.summary: NPR analysis finds conflicting signals from Trump and Israel suggest no unified exit strategy as the war enters its fourth week with diplomatic channels completely closed.

outlets.name: CNN
outlets.headline: Inside Trump's most difficult war decision — boots on the ground in Iran
outlets.summary: CNN reports Trump is reviewing options for ground troops in Iran in daily briefings, even as he publicly denies it. The contradiction with Katz's escalation announcement draws scrutiny.

outlets.name: Washington Post
outlets.headline: Deepening Iran conflict exposes cracks in U.S., Israel objectives
outlets.summary: WaPo analysis details growing divergence between Trump's wind-down signals and Israel's escalation plans, raising questions about strategic coherence.

outlets.name: Fox News
outlets.headline: Iran fires missiles at Diego Garcia as UK allows US to use bases for Hormuz strikes
outlets.summary: Fox focuses on the Diego Garcia IRBM attack as proof of Iranian aggression, and UK's decision to expand US basing access as necessary allied support.

outlets.name: Wall Street Journal
outlets.headline: Trump signals Iran war winding down — allies, critics skeptical
outlets.summary: WSJ notes Trump's winding-down statement came as the Pentagon was deploying more Marines, seeking $200B, and Israel announcing intensified attacks.

outlets.name: NY Post
outlets.headline: Trump: 'Nobody left to talk to' in Iran — mulling historic victory declaration
outlets.summary: NY Post frames Trump's statement as a victory lap, reporting the president told reporters Iran's leadership has been 'eliminated' and there is no one left to negotiate with.

outlets.name: Al Jazeera
outlets.headline: Iran war: What's happening on day 22 of US-Israel attacks?
outlets.summary: Al Jazeera comprehensive Day 22 roundup covers Natanz 2nd strike, Diego Garcia IRBM attack, Trump wind-down, Katz escalation, and Pezeshkian's Nowruz address.

outlets.name: Reuters
outlets.headline: Iran says Natanz struck, fires missiles at Diego Garcia; Trump hints at wind-down
outlets.summary: Reuters covers Natanz as the dominant Day 22 story alongside Iran's unprecedented IRBM attack on the remote US-UK Diego Garcia base in the Indian Ocean.

outlets.name: BBC
outlets.headline: Nowruz in wartime: Iranians mark Persian New Year amid conflict and fear
outlets.summary: BBC focuses on the human dimension of Nowruz 2026 — the first wartime Persian New Year in decades — alongside Pezeshkian's diplomatic proposal.

outlets.name: Times of Israel
outlets.headline: 'Hope and fear': Iranians celebrate Nowruz darkened by losses of war
outlets.summary: Times of Israel covers the dual narrative of Nowruz: Iranians celebrating the new year while mourning war losses under IRGC crackdown on gatherings.

outlets.name: Jerusalem Post
outlets.headline: Wide-scale IDF strikes significantly degrade Iran's ballistic missile production
outlets.summary: Jerusalem Post leads with IDF footage of 5 simultaneous strikes on ballistic missile array, calling it the most significant degradation of Iran's missile capability yet.

outlets.name: Haaretz
outlets.headline: Natanz nuclear site hit again; Israel denies knowledge of strike
outlets.summary: Haaretz focuses on the unusual IDF denial of involvement in the Natanz strike, raising questions about US unilateral operations separate from Israeli coordination.

outlets.name: IRNA (Iran)
outlets.headline: Iran condemns illegal attack on Natanz — nuclear material safe, no radiation leak
outlets.summary: IRNA focuses on Iran's condemnation of the Natanz strike, emphasizing the IAEA's confirmation of no radiation leak. No mention of nuclear program setbacks.

outlets.name: Press TV
outlets.headline: Pezeshkian proposes Islamic Assembly of Middle East to end foreign interference
outlets.summary: Press TV leads with Pezeshkian's Nowruz diplomatic proposal as proof of Iran's peaceful intentions and desire to end the war through regional cooperation, not US dictation.

outlets.name: Tasnim News
outlets.headline: Araghchi: Iran will end war permanently, not accept temporary ceasefire
outlets.summary: Tasnim frames Araghchi's NBC appearance as a demonstration of Iranian strength — rejecting ceasefire proposals and insisting on reparations and a full end to aggression.
